item.title: Preventing Crohn's recurrence, Kono-S and extended mesenteric excision: network meta-analysis category.
item.description: Langenbeck's archives of surgery - Systematic review - IntroductionCrohn's disease (CD) is a complex disease with rising incidence that is usually treated with medical management. However, surgery plays an important role for medically refractive cases,...

item.title: Intestinal endometrosis mimicking Crohn's disease: a rare cause of nonspecific abdominal pain.

item.title: Malignant Transformation of Perianal Fistulas: A Systematic Review of the Literature.
item.description: Journal of surgical oncology - Review - Malignant transformation of a perianal fistula is rare, predominantly associated with chronic fistula. Carcinogenesis is presumably initiated by chronic inflammation, and its mechanism is not clear...

item.title: Capsule retention in Crohn's disease requiring surgical management: two case reports.
item.description: Journal of surgical case reports - Case report - Capsule endoscopy is an established diagnostic modality for small bowel evaluation, particularly in Crohn's disease. Capsule retention remains its most significant complication, especially in patie...

item.title: Crohn's Disease: From Immune Dysregulation to Intestinal Damage.
item.description: Inflammation - Review - Crohn's disease (CD) is a long-lasting, recurring inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) marked by segmental, transmural inflammation that may affect any part of the gastrointestinal tract. Growing evide...

item.title: Impact of Diet and Body Mass Index on the Development of Pouchitis in the First Year After Ileal Pouch-Anal Anastomosis.

item.title: Small bowel obstruction secondary to Strongyloides stercoralis: a rare cause and mimic of inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Sequential Biologic, Small-Molecule, and GLP-1 Analogue Therapy in Postcolectomy Enteritis With High Output Stoma.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Postcolectomy enteritis is a rare but serious complication after colectomy for ulcerative colitis. We report the case of a 71-year-old man with acute severe ulcerative colitis who developed steroid...

item.title: Recent advances in the management of small bowel Crohn's disease.
